taskDescription: Complete the crossword using terms from orbital anatomy.

words.clue: Muscle that abducts the eye, supplied by CN VI
Muscle that depresses the adducted eye, supplied by CN IV
Fissure transmitting CN III, IV, V1, and VI into the orbit
Ring-shaped common origin of the four recti muscles
Syndrome with ptosis, miosis, and anhidrosis from sympathetic interruption
Structure through which the optic nerve and ophthalmic artery enter the orbit
Gland producing tears, located in the superolateral orbit
Nerve supplying most extraocular muscles including levator palpebrae superioris
Misalignment of the eyes due to muscle or nerve imbalance
Pulley-like structure through which the superior oblique tendon passes
